title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old weeping willow tree in Boston Public Garden collapsed, surpassing the typical lifespan of 30-50 years for white willows.
Friends of the Public Garden confirmed the tree was structurally sound during recent inspections and no injuries were reported.
Proactive tree care is emphasized by the Friends of the Public Garden, which manages risks through regular pruning and disease management.

title: These new speed boards in Jamaica Plain, on the Esplanade track cyclists’ speeds, not just cars
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: DCR installed speed boards in Jamaica Plain and Esplanade to track and slow down both cyclists and cars.
These boards collect data on path user volume, aiding officials in assessing activity levels on certain properties.
Additional boards, monitoring mainly cyclists, are placed on Memorial Drive, Neponset River Reservation, and multiple state trails.

title: Thousands demand Wu address street safety after cyclist killed in crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 4,000 individuals, including 2,300 Boston residents, signed a letter urging Mayor Wu to improve street safety.
The Massachusetts Vision Zero Coalition held a vigil for Louisa Gag, a transportation planner killed while cycling, at City Hall Plaza.
Mayor Wu pledged increased enforcement and assigned senior staff to accelerate safety projects after the fatal crash on Tremont Street.

title: Police investigating after man, woman in their 70s found dead in Maine home
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Maine State Police are investigating the deaths of Carolyn Harris, 77, and Kevin Harris, 75, found in their West Bath home.
The Sagadahoc County Sheriff's Department discovered the couple during a welfare check on State Road on July 13.
Autopsies are being conducted to determine the cause of death; police say there's no public safety threat.

title: Multiple issues after getting an SUV back from the body shop
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2024 Honda CR-V EX owner reports electrical issues after body shop repairs, including non-working interior lights and automatic hatch locking.
John Paul suggests the Honda's ADAS system requires recalibration after repairs to restore lost programming functions.
Vehicle owners experiencing similar post-repair issues are advised to contact their insurance for a supplemental claim.

title: Officers shot at during Dorchester traffic stop, police say, prompting outcry from union
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police officers were fired upon during a Dorchester traffic stop; the windshield of their cruiser was hit.
Suspect Rasiel Joel Carbuccia was arrested and charged with multiple firearm offenses and assault in the incident.
Police union urged elected officials to condemn the shooting, emphasizing the risks officers face daily.

title: Woman, 25, dead, driver and 3 children hospitalized in crash on I-495 in Andover
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 25-year-old woman from Lawrence died in a minivan crash on I-495 in Andover.
The driver and three children in the minivan were hospitalized; children had unknown injuries.
The crash involved a sedan; the sedan's driver was not injured. Investigation by State Police is ongoing.

title: 3 longtime tenants to close or move after Alpha Management buys prominent Allston commercial building
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Alpha Management purchased the building at 149-175 Harvard Ave., affecting longtime businesses like the Pet Shop, Ritual Arts, and Regeneration Tattoo.
Tenants are being priced out due to higher rents after the $12.25 million sale, prompting closures or relocations.
Alpha Management plans renovations to attract new tenants while honoring existing leases but faces community concerns about future changes.

title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett will present Doc Rivers at his Hall of Fame induction ceremony.
Doc Rivers will be inducted as a coach, with 1,194 career wins, sixth on the NBA's all-time list.
The Hall of Fame ceremony is on August 15 at Symphony Hall in Springfield with the Class of 2026.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum described the trade of Jaylen Brown to the 76ers as 'weird', marking the end of their nine-year Celtics partnership.
The Boston Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and multiple draft picks, sparking emotional fan reactions.
Jaylen Brown expressed mixed emotions about the trade, stating he's still processing the move to the 76ers, a former playoff rival.
